---
title: "Radiation"
author: "Matheus Ardigueri"
date: "2026-04-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
df_snider <- read_excel("./data/TIFTONRADIATION.xls")

df_snider
```

```{r}
rad_filt <- df_snider %>%
  filter(Year == 2025) %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
   filter(Date >= as.Date("2025-05-01") & Date <= as.Date("2025-09-25")) %>%
  clean_names()

```

```{r}
rad_plot <- ggplot(rad_filt,
                   aes(x = date,
                       y = total_solar_radiation_mj_m_2)) +
  labs(
    x = "Months",
    y = "Total Solar Radiation MJ/m²"
  ) +
  geom_line()

ggsave(filename = "./output/rad_plot.png",
        plot = rad_plot,
       dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
rad_cum <- rad_filt %>%
  mutate(cum_srad = cumsum(total_solar_radiation_mj_m_2), .before = date)
```

```{r}
cum_plot <- ggplot(data = rad_cum,
                   aes(x = date,
                        y=cum_srad)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    x = "Months",
    y = "Cummulative Solar Radiation MJ/m² "
  )

ggsave(filename = "./output/cum_rad.png",
      plot = cum_plot,
      dpi = 600)
```
